my_thoughts: The aim/necessity works: aim = create moon (abstract aim), introduces: necessary act = sacrifice suffering (functional necessity), asserts: incapacity (cannot do without sacrifice). The student asks for purpose (seeking purpose of struggle), which shows they're seeking purpose (struggle). They'll likely ask about causal link next. Hypothesis: purpose questions trigger causal link proposals—testable by tracking whether purpose questions lead to causal link inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student asks for purpose ('What is the purpose of struggling against self-will?')—seeking purpose of struggle via conceptual inquiry and purpose seeking. They're seeking purpose (struggle). The comparison_contrast mechanism defines: aim = create moon (abstract aim), introduces: necessary act = sacrifice suffering (functional necessity), asserts: incapacity (cannot do without sacrifice). Pattern: purpose question → aim/necessity → [next: causal question]. Low confidence because purpose questions can lead to clarification requests or compliance.
visible_response:
You remember how we started.
The aim was to create a centre of gravity, create
moon in ourselves. We cannot do it through self-will.
Creating moon in ourselves is connected with the idea of sacrificing suffering.
